documents.text: Write a 2000-word mini literature review on a biology topic of your choice. The review must include an introduction, body sections, and conclusion. Use three peer-reviewed scientific articles (two primary, one review). Apply Harvard referencing style throughout.
documents.title: Mini Literature Review

documents.text: Introduce the topic (2 marks), Content (2 marks), Conclusion (1 mark), Readability (2 marks), References (3 marks), Research process (15 marks).
documents.title: Literature Review Rubric

nodes.content: Write a 2000-word mini literature review on a biology topic of your choice. The review must include an introduction, body sections, and conclusion. Use three peer-reviewed scientific articles (two primary, one review). Apply Harvard referencing style throughout.

Word count: 2000 words maximum (excluding reference list). Format: Introduction, body paragraphs, conclusion, research process section, reference list. Referencing style: Harvard. Submission: Turnitin, one submission only.
Weight: 25. Due: Week 5, Friday 5pm. Course: BABS1201 Molecules Cells and Genes.
LO1: Locate and evaluate peer-reviewed scientific literature. LO2: Synthesise information from multiple sources. LO3: Communicate scientific information clearly in written form.

Hidden curriculum: 'mini literature review' implies academic register, not conversational tone. 'Peer-reviewed' excludes textbooks and websites. Harvard referencing implies UNSW standard. 'Research process section' implies metacognitive reflection on search strategy. Two primary articles means original research papers, not reviews.

[{"band":"HD","descriptor":"Demonstrates exceptional synthesis of literature with clear critical evaluation and original insight"},{"band":"DN","descriptor":"Demonstrates strong synthesis with consistent critical evaluation"},{"band":"CR","descriptor":"Demonstrates adequate synthesis with some critical evaluation"},{"band":"PS","descriptor":"Demonstrates basic understanding with limited synthesis"},{"band":"FL","descriptor":"Does not demonstrate adequate understanding or synthesis"}]
Question 1. Describe the structure of a eukaryotic cell membrane. (4 marks)
Question 2. Explain the process of DNA replication in prokaryotes. Include a labelled diagram. (6 marks)
Question 3. Compare and contrast mitosis and meiosis. Discuss the significance of each process in the context of genetic diversity. (8 marks)
Question 4. Evaluate the role of epigenetic modifications in gene expression regulation. Use at least two specific examples from the literature. (10 marks)
Question 5. Which of the following is NOT a function of the Golgi apparatus? (a) Protein modification (b) Lipid synthesis (c) DNA replication (d) Vesicle formation (1 mark)
